name: Queen's Edict
descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The wielder's {Counter} Generation +[StateArg4]%. After playing a card, gain {Counter} equal to [StateArg1]% of the wielder's DEF, up to 2 times per turn. If the card played enters {Ultra Space}, the wielder also gains [StateArg2] Aliemus. After releasing Exalt, all enemies lose [StateArg3]% Temporary {STR} equal to the wielder's DEF.
lore:
The exact origins of Wanda are widely debated among the locals of Cambrian.

Some say they have seen Wanda sitting on a giant stone sinking into the lakeside.
A man say she is a pharaoh from Remoushe; he once saw with the same spine needle on a mummy.
Still, some say she is a duchess from the Franco Highlands, who inherited a vast fortune after her four husbands all died in succession.
..

Rumors blew away like the wind, and the villagers continued to farm, weave, and grind.
In those hard years, heavy taxes hung overhead like a razor, cutting off all the budding branches and illusions.
Only... whenever passing by the lake area where the queen resides, the music from the thicket easily captivated everyone's hearts.
That was a melody that shouldn't be here.
It was too pleasant, too lazy, making it seem evil, like a devil's whisper.

"Come, cast all this aside."
"The merciful queen sits at the lake's center, waiting for the service of her blind servants..."

So, on a windless night, the weary villagers put down their tools and decided to satisfy their curious desire.
He circumvented the forest, crossed the small bridge, the path blooming with flowers, not a glance spared.
He saw the green lake.
It connected to the low-hanging starry sky, with an ancient castle rising at the edge.

The thorn-crowned queen sat in the center of the lake, patiently awaiting the pilgrims.
She extended her hand, enjoying the kisses and worship of mere mortals.
The dark clouds dispersed, the bright moon rose.
The queen drove the spine needle into his back.
The paralyzing liquid spread through his veins, and he knelt on the ground, dismembered and gouged of his eyes.

At this point, the heavy gravity from the earth could no longer attract him, all his poetry and dreams were solely tethered to that queen.
He finally welcomed his destiny.
